evidence: Review 1: 'The biggest problem for me, is that the shield can fog up (just like my glasses).'
Review 7: 'If the flap tears off (as happened to me), then you will have to unscrew the battery case and remove the rest of the paper before the display will work.'
behavioral_signals.description: Customers express annoyance with small, persistent issues that disrupt their experience, such as fogging or a minor design flaw that requires extra steps.

behavioral_signals.signal_name: Seeking reliable solutions to avoid repeated problems
evidence: Review 4: 'Cordless tools are easier to handle than corded ones, but my cordless tools always need charging when I need to use them.'
Review 7: 'I’ve owned cheap levels where the bubbles shifted positions when you picked up the level and put it down again in the same spot.'
behavioral_signals.description: They actively choose products that promise consistency and durability to prevent the same issues from happening again.

behavioral_signals.signal_name: Valuing products that simplify or automate tasks
evidence: Review 4: 'Cordless tools are easier to handle than corded ones, but my cordless tools always need charging when I need to use them.'

Review 7: 'This is a very giftable tool, because it comes with a strap-handled zipper case, and has so many extra features. For example, if you use the MODE button, the angle reading can show as degrees, percentage, pitch IN/FT, and mm/M. The display shows “Err” whenever you are holding the level in a way that will give you an incorrect reading.'
behavioral_signals.description: There is a preference for tools that reduce effort or eliminate steps, especially to circumvent known frustrations.

behavioral_signals.signal_name: Prioritizing ease of use and comfort
evidence: Review 1: 'This fits many head sizes, and is comfortable to wear.'
Review 7: 'The bubbles, which I mostly use the level for, are accurate, consistent, and easy to see.'
behavioral_signals.description: Comfort and user-friendly design are highlighted as key benefits, indicating a desire to avoid discomfort or complexity.

evidence: Review 1: 'As germ/virus shields, they are acceptable; as workshop shields, not so much so...'
Review 4: 'Jig saws are great for cutting curved lines... They also cut straight lines and cut at angles; and they cut wood, metal, and other materials...'
Review 7: 'I’m a casual handyperson, so I don’t really need the protractor feature... but it’s fun to go around checking things...'
behavioral_signals.description: Customers appreciate products that serve multiple purposes or adapt to different situations, reducing the need for multiple specialized items.
